summary.leadingMessage: Trust drift is now cross-functional and should be closed as one board-visible remediation workstream.

items.boardQuestion: Which AI governance claims would fail a buyer or investor trust review first?
items.currentPosture: Governance language is coherent, but the live proof set is older than the confidence of the public statement.
items.publicAssertion: Agent governance, rollout, and exception control evidence is review-ready.
items.evidenceReality: Rollback, exception-routing, and evaluation review proof still needs a freshness refresh.
items.driftHeadline: AI governance reads stronger in public than it does in the live proof room.
items.driftSignal: Exception-routing and rollback evidence is older than the trust language implies.
items.missingProof: Fresh operating exports for exception handling, evaluation review, and rollback outcomes
items.headline: AI governance needs fresher proof before it should anchor trust review.
items.narrative: This is an evidence freshness problem more than a positioning problem.
items.nextMove: Refresh the operating evidence and narrow the highest-confidence AI claim until the proof room catches up.

assertionConfidenceAssessment.message: Assertion confidence exists, but the language should narrow before reuse.

evidenceFreshnessAssessment.message: Evidence is aging and should be refreshed before the next review cycle.

ownerContinuityAssessment.message: Owner continuity exists, but follow-through still depends on cross-functional coordination.

contradictionAssessment.message: A contradiction is forming and should be closed before the next review cycle.

exposureAssessment.message: External exposure is too high for the current assertion drift.

items.boardQuestion: Which identity trust claims are closest to clean closure?
items.currentPosture: Identity evidence is strong and mostly current.
items.publicAssertion: Identity reviews, guest access, and mobile protection controls are audit-ready.
items.evidenceReality: One guest-access proof export is aging, but the assertion is otherwise aligned to live evidence.
items.driftHeadline: Identity is the healthiest assertion family, with only minor freshness cleanup left.
items.driftSignal: Guest-review evidence is slightly older than ideal, but ownership is clear.
items.headline: Identity can close quickly with a small evidence refresh.
items.narrative: This is the benchmark lane for a disciplined trust assertion surface.
items.nextMove: Refresh the guest-access export and mark this assertion family ready for external reuse.

assertionConfidenceAssessment.message: Assertion confidence is high enough to survive external review.

evidenceFreshnessAssessment.message: Evidence is aging and should be refreshed before the next review cycle.

ownerContinuityAssessment.message: Owner continuity is strong enough to close drift quickly.

contradictionAssessment.message: No material contradiction is visible between assertion and evidence.

exposureAssessment.message: External exposure is rising faster than evidence confidence.

items.boardQuestion: Where will platform and recovery trust review ask for harder evidence?
items.currentPosture: Narrative strength is ahead of proof depth.
items.publicAssertion: Hosting, release safety, backup, and runtime hardening are connected enough for diligence.
items.evidenceReality: Backup readiness, release rollback, and runtime-hardening proof are still fragmented.
items.driftHeadline: Platform trust needs escalation because the evidence chain is still fragmented.
items.driftSignal: Backup readiness, release rollback, and hosted-validator evidence are still loosely linked.
items.missingProof: One end-to-end packet linking hosting controls to release and recovery proof
items.headline: Platform trust needs escalation because the evidence chain is still fragmented.
items.narrative: The issue is connective proof, not lack of platform surface area.
items.nextMove: Escalate one owner to assemble the hosting-to-recovery proof chain.

assertionConfidenceAssessment.message: Assertion confidence is weaker than the public claim implies.

evidenceFreshnessAssessment.message: Evidence is aging and should be refreshed before the next review cycle.

ownerContinuityAssessment.message: Owner continuity is too weak to keep the trust claim stable.

contradictionAssessment.message: Contradiction load is already visible enough to weaken the assertion.

exposureAssessment.message: External exposure is too high for the current assertion drift.

items.boardQuestion: Which trust assertion should anchor the rest of the external proof layer?
items.currentPosture: Procurement is the strongest lane and can serve as the reference trust claim.
items.publicAssertion: Security questionnaire, trust-center, and proof reuse are buyer-ready.
items.evidenceReality: Questionnaire reuse is strong, but still benefits from a clearer proof index.
items.driftHeadline: This trust assertion is structurally strong and mainly needs ongoing hygiene.
items.driftSignal: Questionnaire reuse still benefits from a more explicit proof index.
items.missingProof: Published proof-coverage index across the trust layer
items.headline: Procurement should anchor the trust-evidence stack.
items.narrative: Use this lane as the operating standard for proof reuse and evidence clarity.
items.nextMove: Publish the proof-coverage index and lock this assertion as the reference lane.

assertionConfidenceAssessment.message: Assertion confidence is high enough to survive external review.

evidenceFreshnessAssessment.message: Evidence is fresh enough to support the public assertion.

ownerContinuityAssessment.message: Owner continuity is strong enough to close drift quickly.

contradictionAssessment.message: No material contradiction is visible between assertion and evidence.

exposureAssessment.message: External exposure is rising faster than evidence confidence.

items.boardQuestion: Which revenue-system claims are still too early for external trust language?
items.currentPosture: The revenue-control story is promising but still early as a unified external assertion.
items.publicAssertion: Reporting, attribution, and release controls already form one diligence-grade revenue control plane.
items.evidenceReality: Cross-surface evidence is thinner than the strength of the integrated-control narrative.
items.driftHeadline: Revenue should be framed as an emerging capability, not a closed trust assertion.
items.driftSignal: Cross-surface evidence is thinner than the strength of the integrated-control narrative.
items.missingProof: One end-to-end evidence packet connecting reporting, attribution, and release controls
items.headline: Revenue controls need a stronger proof chain before external trust reuse.
items.narrative: The surface is valuable, but the integrated control-plane claim should wait.
items.nextMove: Restate the integrated assertion until one linked revenue-control packet exists.

assertionConfidenceAssessment.message: Assertion confidence is weaker than the public claim implies.

evidenceFreshnessAssessment.message: Evidence is aging and should be refreshed before the next review cycle.

ownerContinuityAssessment.message: Owner continuity is too weak to keep the trust claim stable.

contradictionAssessment.message: Contradiction load is already visible enough to weaken the assertion.

exposureAssessment.message: External exposure is too high for the current assertion drift.

items.boardQuestion: Which regulated assertions need provenance reinforcement before reuse?
items.currentPosture: The control stack is compelling, but the proof packet needs stronger provenance and fresher timestamps.
items.publicAssertion: GxP, specimen, CAPA, safety, and narrative review already form a reusable regulated trust packet.
items.evidenceReality: Evidence dates and repeatability notes are weaker than the confidence of the regulated trust narrative.
items.driftHeadline: The regulated trust packet is real, but still needs clearer provenance to feel durable.
items.driftSignal: Evidence dates and repeatability notes are weaker than the current trust narrative.
items.missingProof: Fresh provenance notes and one repeatable regulated packet
items.headline: Regulated controls need a provenance refresh before they read like a durable trust packet.
items.narrative: The surface is there; the external packet needs to look more repeatable and less recent.
items.nextMove: Refresh provenance notes and publish one repeatable regulated packet.

verification: Synthetic trust-assertion data only - no live buyer, board, customer, or partner evidence packets are included.

Scores are modeled to show how Kinetic Gain can compare public assertion confidence, evidence freshness, owner continuity, contradiction load, and exposure pressure in one board-readable trust surface.
All routes are read-only and demonstrate trust-assertion drift monitoring, not production legal, security, or compliance advice.
